name: Charge_Mech

infobox.Systems: Ramming Engines(Brute)EffectFly in a line and slam into the target, pushing it and hurting yourself.(1 self, 2 damage)Upgrade I+1 Damage Each ()Increases damage to self and target by 1.Upgrade II+1 Damage ()Increases damage to target by 1.

sections.Team: The mech is available as part of theZenith Guardsquad.
sections.Strategy:
The Charge Mech has strong damage and decent mobility, thanks to its Ramming Engines weapon; it essentially acts as aCombat Mechwith theDashupgrade by default. However, it also comes with the drawback of damaging itself after hitting a target.
The pilotAbe Isamuis a good choice for the Charge Mech, because his passiveArmoredability protects the Mech from 1 self-damage of its Ramming Engines weapon. With this Mech, you will use his special ability defensively AND offensively.
The weapon Ramming Engines' self damage is also considered an attack against terrain and will cause terrain effect on the tile it ends the attack on; this notably includes lightingforest tilesonfireand breaking cracked tiles. The pilotMafancan be used alternatively to absorb the self-damage with his shield, which prevents the terrain effect from activating as well.
The Charge Mech can be used with other pilots too, such asAriadnewith her additional boost to HP allowing the Mech to survive; or any pilot with theTechnicianability as well. In aCustom Squad, the Charge Mech can also be paired with mechs such as theNano Mechto provide it a way of repairing itself without the need of specific pilot abilities. As long as the self-damage is mitigated, the+1 Damage Eachupgrade should be gotten as soon as it's reasonable, as it is a cheap upgrade for an important increase in damage to ensure that the Charge Mech kills its target.
Despite its good damage output, the Charge Mech lacks potential to resolve multiple targets; it is capable of neutralizing two enemies in one turn with bump damage or by pushing another enemy into another attack, but this requires some luck with enemies lining up. With this in mind, the Charge Mech will benefit from a secondary utility weapon that can let it resolve more targets at once, when Ramming Engines isn't sufficient.
